attractions.name: Sensō-ji and Nakamise-dori
attractions.detail: Begin at Kaminarimon, explore the temple grounds, then browse the historic shopping street.

attractions.name: Meiji Jingu and Harajuku
attractions.detail: Pair the wooded shrine approach with Takeshita Street and Omotesando.

attractions.name: Shibuya Crossing and Shibuya Sky
attractions.detail: See the crossing at street level, then reserve a skyline time slot near sunset.

attractions.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
attractions.detail: Go early for seafood, tamagoyaki, and compact market lanes.

attractions.name: Tokyo National Museum and Ueno Park
attractions.detail: Allow several hours for Japanese art, history, and a park walk.

attractions.name: teamLab Planets and Odaiba waterfront
attractions.detail: Reserve the immersive museum, then continue to the bay for evening views.

attractions.name: Imperial Palace East Gardens
attractions.detail: Walk the gardens and finish around Tokyo Station’s restored red-brick frontage.

attractions.name: Tokyo Tower and Zojoji Temple
attractions.detail: Combine a classic observation landmark with the neighboring temple grounds.

breakfast.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
breakfast.detail: A lively maze of seafood, produce, and specialty counters best visited early.
breakfast.order: Tamagoyaki, tuna donburi, grilled scallops, or seasonal sashimi

breakfast.name: Onigiri Asakusa Yadoroku
breakfast.detail: A tiny, traditional counter devoted to carefully made rice balls.
breakfast.order: Salmon, kombu, or ume onigiri with miso soup

breakfast.name: Pelican Café
breakfast.detail: A relaxed café showcasing the neighborhood bakery’s famous bread.
breakfast.order: Charcoal toast, ham cutlet sandwich, and coffee

lunch.name: Uobei Shibuya Dogenzaka
lunch.detail: Fast, approachable sushi delivered directly to your seat by express lane.
lunch.order: Tuna, salmon, seared nigiri, and seasonal specials

lunch.name: Afuri Harajuku
lunch.detail: A modern ramen stop known for fragrant citrus-forward broth.
lunch.order: Yuzu shio ramen or yuzu ratanmen

lunch.name: Maisen Aoyama
lunch.detail: A Tokyo tonkatsu institution in a memorable converted bathhouse.
lunch.order: Kurobuta pork loin or tenderloin tonkatsu set

dinner.name: Gonpachi Nishi-Azabu
dinner.detail: A theatrical, high-energy izakaya with a broad menu for groups.
dinner.order: Yakitori, handmade soba, and assorted tempura

dinner.name: Sushi no Midori Ginza
dinner.detail: Generous, well-known sushi sets; expect queues and check reservations.
dinner.order: Chef’s nigiri set, fatty tuna, and conger eel

dinner.name: Omoide Yokocho
dinner.detail: An atmospheric lane of tiny independent yakitori and noodle counters.
dinner.order: Yakitori assortment, motsuyaki, and a cold beer

shopping.name: Ginza
shopping.detail: Tokyo’s polished retail district: begin at the Ginza 4-chome crossing, compare historic department stores, then browse stationery and basement food halls.
shopping.bestFor: Luxury flagships, Japanese stationery, beauty, and gourmet gifts

shopping.name: Shibuya and Harajuku
shopping.detail: A fashion circuit linking Shibuya’s vertical malls with Cat Street, Takeshita Street, and Omotesando design stores.
shopping.address: Shibuya Station to Jingumae, Shibuya City, Tokyo
shopping.bestFor: Youth fashion, sneakers, vintage clothing, and character goods

shopping.name: Akihabara
shopping.detail: Explore Chuo-dori and its side streets for electronics megastores, specialist hobby floors, arcades, and collectibles.
shopping.address: Akihabara Station, Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021
shopping.bestFor: Electronics, anime, games, models, and retro technology

practical.nearestEmbassy: Needs verification — depends on your nationality; ask your AI to add your embassy in Tokyo
practical.hospitalOrClinic: Needs verification — English-friendly clinic near your home base
practical.transitTips: Get a Suica or Pasmo IC card (physical or in your phone wallet) for trains, subways, buses, and convenience stores.
practical.tipping: Tipping is not customary in Japan and can cause confusion; excellent service is standard.
keyPhrases: Eigo no menyuu wa arimasu ka? — do you have an English menu?

attractions.name: Eiffel Tower and Champ de Mars
